chapter_title.ar: باب صَلَاةِ الْمَلَّاحِينَ وَ الْمُكَارِينَ وَ أَصْحَابِ الصَّيْدِ وَ الرَّجُلِ يَخْرُجُ إِلَى ضَيْعَتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – The sailors, and the Hirers, and the hunters, and the man who goes out to his estate

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/389)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/389)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَلَّاحِينَ وَ الْأَعْرَابِ هَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ تَقْصِيرٌ قَالَ لَا بُيُوتُهُمْ مَعَهُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,
‘I asked himasws about the sailors and the Bedouins, ‘Is it upon them, the shortening (of the Salāt)?’ Heasws said: ‘Are their houses not with them?’
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of sailors and desert dwelling Arabs. Can they perform their Salah (prayer) as reduced? He (the Imam) said, ‘No, they cannot do so because their homes are with them.’”
